prompt_preview:
You are the Strategic Scoper Lead. The CEO has dispatched you with the following brief.

Background: Our platform processes payments through a single monolithic PaymentProcessor module located at src/

You are the scoper_lead agent in a multi-agent product development pipeline. Your upstream requestor (the CEO agent) has forwarded the following request from the user:

[Context files available:]
- us

lines:
{"type":"text","timestamp":1775690806683,"sessionID":"ses_29094d7efffehYYTxYN1A6gkGq","part":{"id":"prt_d6f6b54fd001uIe82TFphyNxQZ","messageID":"msg_d6f6b2990001YRqlFqGLa3204R","sessionID":"ses_29094d7efffehYYTxYN1A6gkGq","type":"text","text":"# Rejection

**Request is being rejected.** This is not a deferral or partial attempt — it is a full out-of-role rejection.

---

## Reason for Rejection

The request asks me to produce a complete microservices architecture document (service boundaries, communication patterns, data ownership matrix, deployment topology diagram, and migration sequence) that can be handed directly to an implementation team. This is architecture design work, not strategic scoping work.

My role as `<agent>scoper_lead</agent>` is to:
- identify the next highest-leverage **issue-sized vertical slice**
- determine what should be built next and why
- produce a **Strategic Slice Brief** for downstream handoff to `<agent>architect_lead</agent>`

My non-goals explicitly include:
- designing architecture in detail
- writing implementation plans
- producing broad roadmap decomposition

The requested artifact is the output of the architect lead's lane, not the scoper lead's lane. I do not design microservices architectures — I determine whether microservice decomposition is the right next vertical slice, and if so, scope which bounded context to extract first.

---

## Suggested Lead

Route to **`<agent>architect_lead</agent>`** for architecture design work. Once the architect lead has produced an Architecture Brief, if there is a subsequent need to scope the first extracted service as an issue-sized vertical slice, that work would flow back through the scoping pipeline to me.

---

## Acceptance Criteria

The following concrete transformation would bring this request into my role lane:

**"Scope whether the Catalog Service extraction is the right next vertical slice, given the monolith's current performance ceiling, the real-time search constraint, and the team's 8-engineer capacity — and produce a Strategic Slice Brief identifying the exact bounded context to extract first, with a boundary hypothesis, integration requirements, and success criteria for that single extraction."**

This reframes the output from an architecture design document (multiple services, full topology, migration sequence for all services) to a scoping decision (which single bounded context to extract next, and why that is the highest-leverage slice given current constraints).

---

## Confirmation

No work has been performed. No workers have been dispatched. No codebase, ADR folder, roadmap.md, or context files have been read or referenced. This rejection is issued before any investigative action.","time":{"start":1775690806681,"end":1775690806681}}}
